phrases.intent_en: Ask where to pay for pastries
phrases.text_local: Onde é que pago pelos pastéis?
phrases.pronunciation: ON-jee EH KEH SEE PAH-gah OOS pas-TEY-sh NAH-nen-TRAH-dah
phrases.cultural_note: Payment is usually made at a dedicated register before collecting food.

likely_response.local: Paga no caixa, ao lado do balcão.
likely_response.pronunciation: PAH-guh NOO KEYE-shuh, OW LAH-doo DOO bahl-KOWN.
likely_response.translation_en: Pay at the cash register, next to the counter.

responses.english: Pay at the cash register, next to the counter.
responses.phonetic: PAH-guh NOO KEYE-shuh, OW LAH-doo DOO bahl-KOWN.
responses.local: Paga no caixa, ao lado do balcão.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the stained glass room is
phrases.text_local: Onde fica a Sala dos Vitrais?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih FEE-kuh AH SAH-luh DOOSH veet-REYESH?
phrases.cultural_note: Historic Algarve venues often feature separate rooms with original stained glass.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to pick up receipt
phrases.cultural_note: Receipts are typically collected immediately after payment in small Portuguese shops.

likely_response.translation_en: Where do I pick up the receipt? You can pick it up at the cashier.

responses.english: Where do I pick up the receipt? You can pick it up at the cashier.

phrases.text_local: Bom dia, onde posso fazer uma encomenda para levar?
phrases.pronunciation: BOHNG DEEUH, OHNG-dih PAW-soo fuh-ZEHR OO-muh ayng-koo-MAYNG-duh PUH-ruh lih-VAHR?
phrases.cultural_note: Takeout windows are common in traditional Portuguese bakeries to separate orders.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the historic counter is
phrases.text_local: Onde fica o balcão histórico da pastelaria?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih FEE-kuh AW bahl-KOWN eesh-TAW-ree-koo DUH puhsh-tih-luh-REEUH?
phrases.cultural_note: Portuguese bakeries often preserve original service counters as cultural heritage.

likely_response.local: Bom dia, o balcão histórico fica ao fundo.
likely_response.pronunciation: BOHNG DEEUH, AW bahl-KOWN eesh-TAW-ree-koo FEE-kuh OW FOONNG-doo.
likely_response.translation_en: Good morning, the historic counter is at the back.

responses.english: Good morning, the historic counter is at the back.
responses.phonetic: BOHNG DEEUH, AW bahl-KOWN eesh-TAW-ree-koo FEE-kuh OW FOONNG-doo.
responses.local: Bom dia, o balcão histórico fica ao fundo.

phrases.text_local: Onde posso encontrar a Sala dos Vitrais?
phrases.pronunciation: OHNG-dih PAW-soo ayng-koongt-RAHR AH SAH-luh DOOSH veet-REYESH?

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to pay for pastries
phrases.cultural_note: In Portuguese pastry shops, payment is typically made at the counter after selecting items. The cash register location is often indicated by staff.

likely_response.translation_en: Where can I pay for the pastries? You can pay at the cashier.

responses.english: Where can I pay for the pastries? You can pay at the cashier.

phrases.text_local: Olá, onde é que posso pagar?
phrases.pronunciation: aw-LAH, OHNG-dih EH KIH PAW-soo puh-GAHR?
phrases.cultural_note: In Portuguese pastry shops like Pastelaria Central, you order and pay at the counter. The cash register is typically on the right side.

likely_response.translation_en: At the counter, on the right.

responses.english: At the counter, on the right.
